description: The signature of the 'plot' method in '_RegressionPlotter' is incompatible with its base class '_LinearPlotter'.
reasoning: The base class '_LinearPlotter' defines the method signature as 'plot(self, ax)', whereas the subclass '_RegressionPlotter' overrides it as 'plot(self, ax, scatter_kws, line_kws)'. This violates the Liskov Substitution Principle because the subclass cannot be used as a substitute for the base class; any code expecting the base class contract (calling 'plot' with a single argument) will fail with a TypeError if passed an instance of '_RegressionPlotter'.

description: The subclass redefines the data interpretation contract of the base class 'VectorPlotter' for wide-form data.
reasoning: The base class 'VectorPlotter' defines a contract for wide-form data where the independent variable 'x' is mapped from the data index ('@index'). '_CategoricalPlotter' redefines this contract by overriding the 'wide_structure' class attribute to map 'x' from the data columns ('@columns') instead. This change in the semantic interpretation of the input data structure makes the subclass non-substitutable for the base class in generic wide-form data processing contexts.
